title: Segmentation fault when performing read_verilog on large file
body:
When trying to perform read_verilog on a file that contains approx. 115000 lines, yosys crashes with an unspecific segmentation fault. As this file only contains very primitive verilog constructs (no functions, no systemverilog etc.), I assume the crash happens mainly because of the file size. The specialty of the file is the fact that it not only contains one module but rather many module instances and definitions. As it is part of delivered IP, I cannot easily modify it or publish it here. The file contains actual RTL, no netlist.

Maybe it is possible for you to recreate this anyway, let me know if you need further input.
